# 

# Introduction

---

 {#introduction}

The purpose of this project proposal document is to present a system that helps users in their daily Islamic learning. This system will be developed in collaboration with Al-Huda International and will use their corpus of Islamic audio lectures and make it available to the general public. The system will also have multiple AI features to further assist users in their learning. This will include features such as QnA chatbot for precise question answering, automated extraction of short lecture clips, transcriptions for lectures and contextually linked islamic sources for efficient knowledge discovery.

## 1.1 Problem Statement {#1.1-problem-statement}

Al Huda International needs a platform that allows people to listen to their audio lectures. Due to an overload of content on the internet, Islamic Teachers and Students of knowledge usually have trouble finding the exact information needed, they end up scrubbing through hours of content and tons of papers/books. Everyday muslims need a way to get precise answers for their questions but at the same time they need to be sure that the answers they receive have proper sources from the Quran/Hadith. In today's world, people's attention spans have gotten shorter. It's hard to take out 2 hours to listen to a lecture.

## 1.2 Motivation {#1.2-motivation}

The motivation behind this project is to support Muslims in their journey of learning Islam by giving them easy access to authentic lectures and enhancing the learning process through modern technologies. By integrating AI-driven tools such as transcription, question answering, and contextual linking, the system aims to make Islamic knowledge more approachable, engaging, and reliable for a wide audience. Our vision is to establish this platform as the go-to application for anyone seeking to deepen their understanding of Islam, whether they are students of knowledge or everyday learners. Beyond its practical utility, the project also serves a higher spiritual purpose: contributing to Sadaqah Jariyah by helping spread Islam.

## 1.3 Problem Solution {#1.3-problem-solution}

To address these challenges, we propose developing an AI-powered Islamic learning platform that ingests lectures from Al-Huda International, generates accurate transcriptions verified by experts, and enriches them with contextual linking and intelligent retrieval. The platform will provide users with precise answers through a citation-backed Q\&A chatbot, while also offering short, meaningful lecture clips and interactive transcripts for easier learning and sharing. By organizing lectures, Qur’anic verses, Hadith, and related topics into a connected knowledge base, users will be able to seamlessly explore authentic Islamic sources, making the learning process more accessible, engaging, and trustworthy for both students of knowledge and everyday Muslims.

## 1.4 Stakeholders {#1.4-stakeholders}

* **Al-Huda** \- Providing the 30k audio lectures  
* **Teachers of Quran and Hadith** \- Need quick access to sources to prepare lectures.  
* **Students learning Quran and Hadith** \- Need specific lectures to understand the new ayat/hadith they've learnt  
* **Everyday Muslims** \- Need to listen to lectures, and need precise answers to their questions along with the source  
* **Experts from Al-Huda** \- Verifying the extracted transcript, entities and short clips generated by the system  
* **Development Team** \- Responsible for developing the mobile app, and the expert panel.

# 

# Project Description

---

 {#project-description}

## 2.1 Scope {#2.1-scope}

The scope of this project covers the complete workflow for processing and delivering Islamic lectures in a structured, accurate, and user-friendly manner. Lectures will be loaded into the system and processed through a multilingual speech-to-text pipeline capable of handling Urdu, Arabic, and English. The generated transcripts will then be routed to an admin panel, where experts from Al-Huda will review them for accuracy, make corrections when needed, and formally approve them. Only after this expert approval will the transcripts become available for use in other parts of the system, ensuring authenticity and reliability.

Once verified, transcripts will power interactive playback, allowing users to listen to lectures with timestamped transcripts and jump directly to specific points of interest. Teachers will benefit from this functionality by quickly locating accurate sources that can be cited in their lectures. Students will gain the ability to connect newly learned ayat or hadith to relevant explanations, enabling them to see where this knowledge fits into the larger context of Islam. Everyday Muslims, who may have limited time, will be able to listen to short, meaningful clips instead of full-length lectures.

From these transcripts, key entities such as Qur’anic verses, hadith, and topics will be extracted using natural language processing and stored in a knowledge graph, enabling contextual linking across lectures and sources. Embeddings of the transcripts will also be stored in a vector database to support semantic search and citation-backed question answering. This allows teachers, students, and Muslims to ask natural language questions and receive precise answers with references to verified ayat, hadith, or transcript segments.

In addition, topic segmentation will generate short, independent clips from long lectures, giving everyday Muslims a convenient way to consume knowledge in smaller, focused segments. These clips can be easily shared across platforms such as WhatsApp and social media, extending the reach of authentic Islamic content. Users will also benefit from personalization features such as resuming playback where they left off and bookmarking lectures or clips for later study.

The final solution will consist of a **Flutter-based mobile application** for end users to access lectures, transcripts, and clips, and a **Next.js admin panel** for expert review, curation, and validation of content. A secure backend API will manage data workflows, content approval, and AI-powered features such as transcription, knowledge graph population, and semantic search.

To maintain focus and accuracy, certain features are explicitly excluded from this project. There will be no crowdsourced corrections; all verification will be carried out exclusively by Al-Huda experts. The platform will not include a recommendation engine or real-time live lecture streaming. This ensures that the project remains centered on verified, authentic, and trustworthy knowledge delivery.

 ![][image2]

## Figure 2.1: Conceptual Diagram

## 2.2 Modules {#2.2-modules}

### 2.2.1: Lecture Discovery, Playback & Interactive Transcripts {#2.2.1:-lecture-discovery,-playback-&-interactive-transcripts}

The app provides users with seamless access to lectures through audio playback and synchronized transcripts.

* The system shall allow users to browse and discover multiple lectures across different series.  
* The system shall allow users to play, pause, rewind, and fast-forward any lecture audio.  
* The system shall allow users to download full lectures for offline playback.  
* The system shall display time-synchronized transcripts alongside audio.  
* The system shall allow users to tap transcript lines to jump to the exact point in the audio.

### 2.2.2: Transcription & Expert Review {#2.2.2:-transcription-&-expert-review}

An expert ensures lectures are accurately transcribed and verified by qualified experts before public release.

* The system shall transcribe uploaded audio lectures using a Speech-to-Text (STT) pipeline  
* The system shall display generated transcripts in the Admin Panel for expert review.  
* The Admin Panel shall allow experts to approve or reject transcripts.  
* The Admin Panel shall allow experts to edit transcript text before approval.  
* Only approved transcripts shall be available to end users or used in downstream features (Q\&A, KG, clips).

### 2.2.3: Question Answering (Q\&A) with Citations {#2.2.3:-question-answering-(q&a)-with-citations}

Allows users to ask natural-language questions and receive precise, citation-backed answers from verified content.

* The system shall store approved transcripts as embeddings in a Vector Database for semantic search.  
* The system shall allow users to submit natural language queries (in English/Urdu/Arabic).  
* The system shall return answers that are:  
  * Accurate, based only on verified transcripts.  
  * Citation-backed, with links to Ayah, Hadith, or transcript segments.  
  * Scoped to a specific series of lectures  
* The system shall allow users to tap a citation to view the transcript segment and play from that timestamp.

### 2.2.4: Knowledge Graph Construction & Navigation {#2.2.4:-knowledge-graph-construction-&-navigation}

Organizes and links Islamic sources for contextual exploration and reverse lookup.

* The system shall extract Ayat, Hadith, and key topics from approved transcripts.  
* Experts shall validate extracted entities before inclusion in the Knowledge Graph.  
* The system shall populate a Knowledge Graph linking:  
  * Lectures ↔ Ayat  
  * Lectures ↔ Hadith  
  * Topics ↔ Lectures  
* The system shall allow users to:  
  * View all Ayat/Hadith mentioned in a lecture.  
  * Select an Ayah/Hadith to see all other lectures that reference it.

### 2.2.5: Short-Form Content Generation {#2.2.5:-short-form-content-generation}

Delivers concise, shareable knowledge through clips derived from long lectures.

* The system shall automatically propose 30–120 second clips from transcripts using topic segmentation.  
* Experts shall review and approve clips before they are published.  
* The system shall allow users to play short clips within the app.  
* The system shall allow users to share clips externally

## 2.3 Tools and Technologies {#2.3-tools-and-technologies}

**Frontend:** Admin Panel \- Next.js, App \- Flutter  
**Backend:** Python (FastAPI)  
**AI:** OpenAI/Whisper, Gemini  
**Database:** PostgreSQL, GraphDB, PineconeDB  
**Deployment:** Docker, Digital Ocean

## 2.5 Timeline {#2.5-timeline}

| Iteration \# | Time Frame | Tasks/Modules |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **1** | **Sept \- Dec** | 1.ERD for lectures and transcriptions, wireframes for both app and admin panel. 2\. ASR pipeline \+ timestamped transcripts 3\. Audio playback |
| **2** | **Jan \- Feb** | Ayah/Hadith Pages Design Short Clip Generation GraphDB Population Lecture sources linking and vice versa |
| **3** | **March- April** | Search UI Design Embed Transcripts and store in vectorDB Complete QnA chatbot Implementation |
| **4** | **May \- June** | Testing of app \+ Bug Fixes Deployment of app on AppStore and Playstore Finalise Documentation |
